hook: Private therapy in the UK runs about fifty to ninety pounds a session. People feel that.
vo: In the UK, a private therapy session usually costs somewhere between fifty and ninety pounds for around fifty minutes.
That's not a small thing to ask of someone who's struggling.
When a therapist keeps more of what they charge, they can think harder about what they charge.
Faresay is free to start. No commission on sessions. Therapists set their own prices and keep their own clients.
onscreen_text: ...tends to mean fairer for the client
cta: Fair for the therapist usually means fairer for the client too.
broll_notes: Close-up of a phone screen showing a clean booking or payment interface; cut to hands typing a modest figure into a price field; end on a calm, unbranded desk — notebook, cup of tea, nothing clinical.
